executive_verdict.primary_failure_mode: Loop ownership leakage and duplicate recovery/watchdog layers were able to hide behind green cron state.
executive_verdict.most_urgent_fix: Consolidate the unblocker path into one clearly-owned loop and remove stale disabled-docs prompt contamination after today's system-health repair.

findings.title: System health monitor had a hidden cross-owner repair path into marketing
findings.mechanism: The system repair loop was reading marketing-state artifacts and triggering marketing-active-loop itself, so a supposedly generic health monitor could mutate marketing behavior from outside the marketing owner stack.
findings.root_cause: Runtime repair authority was encoded in system-level monitor code instead of staying inside the marketing owner/watchdog stack.
findings.recommended_fix: Fixed this run by narrowing health_monitor.py back to cron/runtime issue detection and timeout-based reruns only; keep marketing-state interpretation inside marketing-owned loops.

findings.title: The unblocker path is duplicated and mislabeled as a Reddit follow-up
findings.mechanism: Two enabled cron jobs run the same unblocker test + agents/unblocker/run.py path, but one is named reddit-autopost-followup even though it performs generic blocked-channel recovery across non-Reddit channels.
findings.root_cause: Owner boundaries between marketing posting and generic channel recovery were never normalized after the unblocker loop was introduced.
findings.recommended_fix: Either consolidate both jobs under a single unblocker-owned schedule or split a truly Reddit-specific follow-up out of the generic unblocker path and rename the remaining job accordingly.

findings.title: A disabled docs job still carries a live-sounding permanent watchdog prompt
findings.mechanism: The disabled docs-stack-aggressive-10min-self-heal job is clearly marked disabled in metadata, but its payload still presents itself as the permanent docs watchdog, which can contaminate future reactivation or audits.
findings.root_cause: Stale disabled job payloads were retained without being normalized after the independent verifier-supervisor took over.
findings.recommended_fix: Rewrite the disabled job payload to explicit legacy/deactivated language or remove the stale job once retention is no longer needed.

findings.title: The self-improvement registry does not cover all live owner/watchdog loops
findings.mechanism: self_improvement_loops.json registers docs, marketing, and Ralph-Site, but not the live system-health monitor, unblocker review path, or the architecture watchdog itself, leaving whole-system auditability partially prompt-defined instead of registry-defined.
findings.root_cause: Loop registration standards were applied unevenly as new runtime-owned loops were added.
findings.recommended_fix: Register each live self-improving/repair loop with explicit owner, artifacts, and verifier contract, or mark it explicitly non-self-improving so the boundary is machine-auditable.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Consolidate unblocker ownership and remove the misleading Reddit-branded duplicate
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: It is the largest remaining live overlap and can still cause repeated recovery noise and unclear ownership.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: One recovery owner, less duplicated churn, clearer reporting boundaries.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Normalize or retire the disabled docs aggressive self-heal payload
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: The docs stack is currently healthy, so this is the cleanest moment to remove stale prompt contamination without incident pressure.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Less audit confusion and lower risk of reviving the wrong watchdog persona later.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Expand self_improvement_loops.json into a complete registry for live repair/watchdog loops
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Today’s architecture audit exposed real gaps between live cron topology and the declared loop map.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Machine-auditable ownership, clearer verifier requirements, and less prompt-only governance.

repairs_applied_this_run.change: Removed marketing artifact inspection and cross-owner trigger logic from the system health monitor so it now stays within cron/runtime health scope.
proof: python3 /home/mistlight/.openclaw/workspace/agents/system/health_monitor.py -> ✅ System health OK - no issues detected

independent_verification.summary: Independent subagent verification passed after the repair and confirmed the health monitor no longer reads marketing audit/adoption/sync artifacts.

highest_risk_unresolved_issue.title: Duplicate unblocker scheduling with misleading Reddit ownership
highest_risk_unresolved_issue.why: It is still live, enabled, and capable of producing duplicate recovery activity and ownership confusion across marketing vs unblocker boundaries.
